$comment: River configs for scripts/import-nhd-rivers.ts. Adding a river = adding an entry here (or passing a different config file: npx tsx scripts/import-nhd-rivers.ts path/to/config.json). Find nhdFeatureId on the USGS National Map (NHD Permanent Identifier). state/timezone/riverType feed the multi-region columns from migration 00145.
$buffalo_nhd_todo: The Buffalo entry below has nhdFeatureId 'UNKNOWN' — the National Map host (hydro.nationalmap.gov) was blocked by network policy when this was drafted. To fill it, query the NHDPlus_HR flowline layer for the named river and take the permanent_identifier of the main-stem segment(s): https://hydro.nationalmap.gov/arcgis/rest/services/NHDPlus_HR/MapServer/2/query?where=gnis_name%3D%27Buffalo+River%27+AND+reachcode+LIKE+%2711%25%27&outFields=permanent_identifier,gnis_name,reachcode,lengthkm&returnGeometry=false&f=json  (reachcode LIKE '11%' restricts to the Arkansas-White HUC region so you don't pick up a Buffalo River in another state). Note: the Buffalo is many flowline segments; a single permanent_identifier is one reach only. The import script fetches just features[0], so for a full-river geometry the Buffalo will likely need the seed-file path (scripts/seed-rivers.ts) rather than a single-ID NHD pull — same as any long multi-segment river. Until then this entry is a scaffold: import-nhd-rivers will log 'NHD fetch failed' for it and skip, which is harmless.

rivers.name: Meramec River
rivers.description: Popular float stream in east-central Missouri, known for scenic bluffs and easy access.

rivers.name: Current River
rivers.description: One of the first National Scenic Riverways, featuring crystal-clear spring-fed waters.

rivers.name: Eleven Point River
rivers.description: Wild and scenic river with remote float sections and abundant wildlife.

rivers.name: Jacks Fork River
rivers.description: Major tributary of the Current River, part of the Ozark National Scenic Riverways.

rivers.name: Niangua River
rivers.description: Popular central Missouri float stream with moderate flow and scenic beauty.

rivers.name: Big Piney River
rivers.description: Clear Ozark stream with excellent smallmouth bass fishing.

rivers.name: Huzzah Creek
rivers.description: Tributary of the Meramec, popular for shorter float trips.

rivers.name: Courtois Creek
rivers.description: Beautiful Meramec tributary with clear water and gravel bars.

rivers.name: Buffalo National River
rivers._nhdNote: See $buffalo_nhd_todo above. Egress to hydro.nationalmap.gov was policy-blocked at draft time; fill permanent_identifier from the National Map query, or import geometry via the seed path since the Buffalo is a long multi-segment main stem.
rivers.description: America's first National River (1972): a free-flowing, rain-driven Ozark river draining the Boston Mountains of northwest Arkansas. Flashy and seasonal in its upper reaches, near year-round floatable in its lower reaches. Managed by the National Park Service.
